title: Repository Pattern
objectives: Combine remote + local sources behind one interface
Enable offline-first via the same API

sections.markdown: A Repository is an abstraction over data sources. It exposes domain-shaped operations (`getUser(id)`) while hiding whether the data comes from network, cache, DB, or a mix.

The Repository is where **offline-first** lives. Callers see one API; internally it reads-through/writes-through to cache and network, exposing a `Stream` so the UI updates when either source changes. Domain never touches Dio or Room.
Leaking data-layer types (DTOs, HTTP responses, DB entities) through the repository interface. Return **domain models** — mappers convert at the boundary.
One repository per aggregate/feature, not one per table. Keep interfaces small; put mappers and caching policy inside the implementation.

flashcards.back: Data sources (network/DB/cache) behind a domain-shaped interface.
The repo reads/writes cache first and syncs remote, so the UI works with or without network.
Data-layer types (DTOs, HTTP responses, DB rows) — always return domain models.

quiz.explain: Domain code calls the interface; implementations pick the source.

options: Uses cache + network behind one API, syncing both
quiz.explain: Reads-through/writes-through to keep the UI usable offline.
Mappers translate DTOs/rows into domain models at the boundary.

challenges.text: Wrap a Retrofit/Dio call behind a Repository interface returning a domain model.
Add Hive/Room caching with read-through fallback.
Expose a Stream from the repo that emits on network OR cache updates.
Add conflict resolution (last-write-wins vs merge) across cache + remote.

interviewQuestions: What is a Repository and what does it hide?
One repository per table or per aggregate?
How do you test a repository?
